title: System architecture to mitigate memory imprinting
abstract: A method and architecture for mitigating memory imprinting in electronic system volatile memory. At system power-up, a bus mode register control determines whether to operate the current power cycle in normal mode or inversion mode, with an objective of equal amounts of time in each mode over the system's lifecycle. A bi-directional data bus inverter is positioned between a system processor and volatile memory. When the system is running in inversion mode, data from non-volatile memory is inverted (0's and 1's are swapped) when copied to volatile memory, and the data bus inverter rectifies all data bits flowing in/out of the processor. By balancing the time spent by individual memory addresses in high and low voltage states, the system minimizes differences in memory cell stresses, thus reducing memory imprinting effects. The same concept applied to other architectures, such as internal processor cache memory, and FPGA configuration memory, is also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method for mitigating memory imprinting in volatile memory of a computer system, said method comprising: providing a computer system with a data bus inverter module located on a data bus between a processor and the volatile memory, and a bus mode control module in communication with the data bus inverter module; determining, by the bus mode control module at system power- up, whether to select a normal mode or an inversion mode for a current power cycle; inverting, when operating in the inversion mode, certain memory address contents when copying system non-volatile memory to the volatile memory at system power-up, where inverting includes swapping values of logical ones and zeroes; running an application program by the processor; inverting, by the data bus inverter module when operating in the inversion mode, data bits from the certain memory addresses of the volatile memory flowing in and out of the processor on the data bus; tracking, by the bus mode control module, cumulative system times operating in the normal mode and the inversion mode; and writing the cumulative system times to a bus mode non-volatile memory module, at system power-down, for use in determining which mode to select at a next system power-up. 2. The method according to claim 1 wherein the certain memory addresses include memory addresses containing one or more of boot code, application code and an interrupt service routine (ISR) handler. 3. The method according to claim 1 wherein determining whether to operate a current power cycle in a normal mode or an inversion mode includes reading the cumulative system times from the bus mode non-volatile 

 memory module and setting the current power cycle to a mode with less cumulative time. 4. The method according to claim 1 wherein the bus mode control module communicates a bus mode control bit and a processor read enable flag to the data bus inverter module, where the bus mode control bit designates the normal or the inversion mode and the processor read enable flag indicates when the processor is reading data from the volatile memory. 5. The method according to claim 4 wherein the data bus inverter module enables one of two directional pass-through gates or one of two directional inverter gates based on the bus mode control bit and the processor read enable flag. 6. The method according to claim 1 wherein the computer system is an embedded control system in a vehicle, an aircraft or a machine. 7. A computer system having volatile memory imprint mitigation, said computer system comprising: a processor receiving input from a system clock; a system volatile memory module in communication with the processor via an address bus, a control bus and a data bus; a system non-volatile memory module in communication with the volatile memory module and the processor via the buses; a data bus inverter module located on the data bus between the processor and the volatile memory module; and a bus mode control module in communication with the data bus inverter module, where the bus mode control module is configured to determine at system power-up whether to operate a current power cycle in a normal mode or an inversion mode, and where the data bus inverter module inverts, when operating in the inversion mode, certain memory addresses when data is being copied 

 from the system non-volatile memory module to the system volatile memory module at system power-up, where inverting includes swapping values of logical ones and zeroes, and the data bus inverter module also inverts, when operating in the inversion mode, data bits from the certain memory addresses of the volatile memory module flowing in and out of the processor on the data bus while the processor is running an application program. 8. The computer system according to claim 7 wherein the bus mode control module is further configured to track cumulative system times operating in the normal mode and the inversion mode, and write the cumulative system times to a bus mode non-volatile memory module, periodically and at system power-down, for use in determining which mode to select at a next system power-up. 9. The computer system according to claim 8 wherein the bus mode control module determines whether to operate the current power cycle in the normal mode or the inversion mode by reading the cumulative system times from the bus mode non-volatile memory module and setting the current power cycle to a mode with less cumulative time. 10. The computer system according to claim 7 wherein the certain memory addresses include memory addresses containing one or more of boot code, application code and an interrupt service routine (ISR) handler. 11. The computer system according to claim 7 wherein the bus mode control module communicates a bus mode control bit and a processor read enable flag to the data bus inverter module, where the bus mode control bit designates the normal mode or the inversion mode and the processor read enable flag indicates when the processor is reading data from the volatile memory module. 12. The computer system according to claim 11 wherein the data bus inverter module enables one of two directional pass-through gates or one of two directional inverter gates based on the bus mode control bit and the processor read enable flag. 13. The computer system according to claim 12 wherein the data bus inverter module enables a processor-to-memory pass-through gate when in the normal mode and the processor is not reading, enables a memory-to- processor pass-through gate when in the normal mode and the processor is reading, enables a processor-to-memory inversion gate when in the inversion mode and the processor is not reading, and enables a memory-to-processor inversion gate when in the inversion mode and the processor is reading. 14. The computer system according to claim 7 wherein the bus mode control module is configured to receive inputs from the system clock, the address bus, the control bus and the data bus. 15. The computer system according to claim 7 wherein the computer system is an embedded control system in a vehicle, an aircraft or a machine. 16. A memory imprint mitigation system for a processorâs memory device, said system comprising: a data bus inverter module located on a data bus between a processor and the processorâs memory device; and a bus mode control module in communication with the data bus inverter module, where the bus mode control module is configured to determine at power-up whether to operate a current power cycle of the system in a normal mode or an inversion mode, and communicate the normal mode or the inversion mode to the data bus inverter module, and where the data bus inverter module inverts, when operating in the inversion mode, data bits flowing on the data bus while the processor is 

 running an application program, where inverting consists of swapping values of logical ones and zeroes. 17. The system according to claim 16 wherein the processorâs memory device is a system volatile memory module which is external to the processor and attached to a common circuit card assembly with the processor, and the data bus inverter module operates on data bits flowing in and out of the processor. 18. The system according to claim 16 wherein the processorâs memory device is a cache memory module which is fabricated on a same integrated circuit die as the processor, and the data bus inverter module operates on data bits flowing in and out of the cache memory module. 19. The system according to claim 16 wherein the bus mode control module is further configured to track cumulative system times operating in the normal mode and the inversion mode, and write the cumulative system times to a bus mode non-volatile memory module, periodically and at system power- down, for use in determining which mode to select at a next system power-up, when the bus mode control module selects a mode with less cumulative time. 20. The system according to claim 16 wherein the bus mode control module communicates a bus mode control bit and a processor read enable flag to the data bus inverter module, where the bus mode control bit designates the normal mode or the inversion mode and the processor read enable flag indicates when the processor is reading data from the volatile memory, and the data bus inverter module enables one of two directional pass-through gates or one of two directional inverter gates based on the bus mode control bit and the processor read enable flag.
